records.ety: [F. composition, fr. L. compositio. See Composite.]

defs: The act or art of composing, or forming a whole or integral, by placing together and uniting different things, parts, or ingredients.
The invention or combination of the parts of any literary work or discourse, or of a work of art; as, the composition of a poem or a piece of music.
The art or practice of so combining the different parts of a work of art as to produce a harmonious whole; also, a work of art considered as such. See 4, below.
The act of writing for practice in a language, as English, Latin, German, etc.
The setting up of type and arranging it for printing.
The state of being put together or composed; conjunction; combination; adjustment.
quotes: View them in composition with other things.

defs: A mass or body formed by combining two or more substances; as, a chemical composition.
quotes: A composition that looks . . . like marble.

defs: A literary, musical, or artistic production, especially one showing study and care in arrangement; -- often used of an elementary essay or translation done as an educational exercise.

quotes: There is no composition in these news That gives them credit.

defs: Mutual agreement to terms or conditions for the settlement of a difference or controversy; also, the terms or conditions of settlement; agreement.
quotes: Thus we are agreed: I crave our composition may be written.

defs: The adjustment of a debt, or avoidance of an obligation, by some form of compensation agreed on between the parties; also, the sum or amount of compensation agreed upon in the adjustment.
quotes: Compositions for not taking the order of knighthood.
Cleared by composition with their creditors.
The investigation of difficult things by the method of analysis ought ever to precede the method of composition.
